god.position: Trinitarian monotheism. God is one Being in three Persons: Father, Son, Holy Spirit.

jesus.position: Fully God and fully man. Virgin birth, sinless life, atoning death, bodily resurrection, second coming.

salvation.position: By grace alone through faith alone. Eternal security (once saved, always saved) in most Baptist traditions.

scripture.position: Bible alone (sola scriptura). 66 books, inspired, inerrant, sufficient. No creeds above Scripture.

afterlife.position: Heaven for believers, hell for unbelievers. Bodily resurrection. Views vary on millennium.

creation.position: God as Creator. Views range from young earth to old earth to framework. No official position required.

angels.position: Real spiritual beings. Satan is a fallen angel. Spiritual warfare is real.

nephilim.position: Views vary. Some hold angel view (sons of God = angels), others Sethite view. Not a test of fellowship.

flood.position: Historical global flood. Noah's Ark literal. Most affirm worldwide.

mary.position: Virgin birth affirmed. Mary honored but not venerated or prayed to. One mediator: Christ.

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion only. Not salvific — an ordinance, not a sacrament.

communion.position: Memorial/ordinance view. Bread and cup are symbols. Not a means of grace.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Indwells believers. Gifts vary by tradition (cessationist to continuationist).

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism per Nicene Creed.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Affirms Chalcedonian definition. Central to Mass and sacraments.

salvation.position: Faith + works + sacraments. Grace through the Church. Justification is a process, not a one-time event. Purgatory for purification.

scripture.position: Bible + Sacred Tradition + Magisterium. 73 books (includes Apocrypha/Deuterocanon). Scripture not sufficient alone.

afterlife.position: Heaven, hell, and purgatory. Saints can intercede. Prayers for the dead are effective.

creation.position: God as Creator. Open to theistic evolution. No required position on age of earth.

angels.position: Rich angelology. Guardian angels. Archangels Michael, Gabriel, Raphael. Hierarchies of angels.

nephilim.position: Generally holds Sethite interpretation (sons of God = line of Seth). Supernatural view not common.

flood.position: Historical event but views vary on extent. Some allow local flood interpretation.

mary.position: Immaculate Conception, perpetual virginity, Assumption, Queen of Heaven, Co-Redemptrix (debated), Mediatrix of graces.

baptism.position: Sacrament that removes original sin. Infant baptism by pouring/sprinkling. Necessary for salvation.

communion.position: Transubstantiation — bread and wine literally become body and blood of Christ. Central act of worship (Mass).

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Proceeds from Father and Son (Filioque). Works through sacraments and Church.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism. Essence-energies distinction. God's essence unknowable, His energies experienced.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Chalcedonian. Central to liturgy and theosis.

salvation.position: Theosis (divinization) — becoming partakers of divine nature. Synergy of grace and human will. No penal substitution emphasis.

scripture.position: Bible + Holy Tradition + Church Councils. 76+ books. Scripture interpreted through Tradition.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell as experiences of God's presence/absence. No purgatory. Prayers for dead. Toll-house theory (debated).

creation.position: God as Creator. Generally open on mechanism. Church Fathers held various views.

angels.position: Elaborate angelology. Nine choirs of angels (Pseudo-Dionysius). Icons of angels venerated.

nephilim.position: Sethite view dominant. Angel interpretation less common in Orthodox tradition.

flood.position: Historical event. Liturgical references. Not dogmatically defined as global vs. local.

mary.position: Theotokos (God-bearer). Ever-virgin. Dormition (fell asleep, was assumed). Highly venerated. Intercessor.

baptism.position: Triple immersion. Infants baptized. Sacrament of initiation with chrismation and communion together.

communion.position: Real presence — a mystery. Not transubstantiation (Western term avoided). Bread and wine are truly body and blood.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Proceeds from Father alone (rejects Filioque). Invoked through epiclesis.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism per Augsburg Confession.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Communicatio idiomatum strongly affirmed.

salvation.position: By grace alone through faith alone (sola fide, sola gratia). Justification is forensic declaration. Simul justus et peccator.

scripture.position: Sola scriptura. Bible is sole authority. Book of Concord as faithful exposition. 66 books canonical.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. No purgatory. Soul sleep debated among some Lutherans.

creation.position: Traditional Lutherans affirm six-day creation. LCMS young earth. ELCA more open.

angels.position: Angels serve God and believers. Satan is real. Spiritual warfare acknowledged.

nephilim.position: Luther leaned toward Sethite view but acknowledged angel view. Not a confessional issue.

flood.position: Global flood affirmed by confessional Lutherans.

mary.position: Theotokos affirmed. Perpetual virginity held by Luther but not required. Not prayed to. One mediator.

baptism.position: Sacrament that works forgiveness of sins. Infant baptism. Baptismal regeneration taught. Means of grace.

communion.position: Real presence — 'in, with, and under' bread and wine (sacramental union). Not transubstantiation or mere symbol.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Works through Word and sacrament (means of grace).

god.position: Trinitarian. Sovereignty of God emphasized. Westminster Confession. TULIP soteriology.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Prophet, Priest, and King. Mediator of the covenant of grace.

salvation.position: Unconditional election. Irresistible grace. Perseverance of the saints. Monergistic. Predestination.

scripture.position: Sola scriptura. Westminster Confession as subordinate standard. 66 books.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. General resurrection. Final judgment. Amillennialism common.

creation.position: God as Creator. Views range from young earth (PCA) to framework hypothesis. Westminster allows variety.

angels.position: Angels and demons real. Elect angels and fallen angels. Satan under God's sovereignty.

nephilim.position: Sethite view more common in Reformed tradition. Calvin held Sethite view.

flood.position: Historical global flood. Covenant with Noah foundational to covenant theology.

mary.position: Virgin birth affirmed. Mary honored but not venerated. No perpetual virginity. One mediator.

baptism.position: Sacrament (sign and seal of covenant). Infant baptism by sprinkling. Covenant children included.

communion.position: Spiritual real presence. Christ truly present spiritually (not physically). More than mere symbol.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Effectual calling. Internal testimony to Scripture. Cessationism common.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism. Prevenient grace emphasized — God enables all to respond.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Universal atonement — Christ died for all people.

salvation.position: By grace through faith. Prevenient, justifying, and sanctifying grace. Entire sanctification (holiness) possible.

scripture.position: Bible is primary authority. Wesleyan Quadrilateral: Scripture, tradition, reason, experience.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. Bodily resurrection. Final judgment.

creation.position: God as Creator. No official position on mechanism or age.

angels.position: Real spiritual beings. Standard Protestant angelology.

nephilim.position: Not a major focus. Views vary.

flood.position: Historical event. Views vary on extent.

mary.position: Virgin birth affirmed. Mary respected but not venerated.

baptism.position: Sacrament. Infant and believer baptism. Sprinkling, pouring, or immersion. Prevenient grace.

communion.position: Real spiritual presence. Open communion. Means of grace. Wesley emphasized frequent communion.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Source of sanctification. Empowers for holy living.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism. Nicene and Apostles' Creeds.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. 39 Articles affirm classical Christology.

salvation.position: By grace through faith. Good works follow as fruit. Via media between Catholic and Protestant.

scripture.position: Bible contains all things necessary for salvation. Tradition and reason also valued. Three-legged stool.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. Bodily resurrection. Wide range of views within communion.

creation.position: God as Creator. Wide range accepted. Many embrace theistic evolution.

angels.position: Real spiritual beings. Book of Common Prayer references angels.

nephilim.position: Not a focus. Views vary widely.

flood.position: Views vary from literal global to mythological. Broad church allows range.

mary.position: Theotokos acknowledged. High-church Anglicans may venerate Mary. Low-church Protestant view.

baptism.position: Sacrament. Infant baptism primary. Sprinkling or pouring. Baptismal regeneration in some traditions.

communion.position: Real presence affirmed but mechanism left undefined. Receptionism common. Central to worship.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Charismatic renewal present in some Anglican churches.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism (most). Some Oneness Pentecostals are modalist.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Baptizer in the Holy Spirit. Coming King.

salvation.position: By grace through faith. Repentance, water baptism, Spirit baptism. Some require tongues as evidence.

scripture.position: Bible as authoritative Word of God. Inerrant. Also value prophetic words and direct revelation (subordinate to Scripture).

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. Pre-tribulation rapture common. Premillennialism dominant.

creation.position: Generally young earth creationist. Six literal days.

angels.position: Active angelology. Angelic encounters expected. Spiritual warfare emphasized. Binding and loosing.

nephilim.position: Angel view common. Spiritual warfare worldview supports supernatural interpretation.

flood.position: Global flood affirmed. Literal historical event.

mary.position: Virgin birth affirmed. Mary not venerated. Focus on Holy Spirit, not Mary.

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion. In Jesus' name (Oneness) or Trinitarian formula.

communion.position: Ordinance/memorial. Symbolic. Not central to worship compared to Spirit-led praise.

holyspirit.position: Central doctrine. Baptism in the Holy Spirit with evidence of tongues. Gifts active today. Power for witness.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism. 16 Fundamental Truths.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Savior, Healer, Baptizer, Coming King (Fourfold Gospel).

salvation.position: By grace through faith. Initial evidence of Spirit baptism is tongues.

scripture.position: Bible is inspired, infallible Word of God. Supreme authority.

afterlife.position: Heaven, hell. Pre-tribulation rapture. Premillennial return of Christ.

creation.position: God as Creator. Generally young earth. Rejects macroevolution.

angels.position: Real beings. Spiritual warfare active. Deliverance ministry common.

nephilim.position: Angel view widely held. Compatible with spiritual warfare emphasis.

flood.position: Global flood. Historical event.

mary.position: Virgin birth affirmed. Not venerated.

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion in Trinitarian formula.

communion.position: Memorial ordinance.

holyspirit.position: Baptism in the Holy Spirit subsequent to salvation. Tongues as initial physical evidence. All gifts for today.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism. New Testament pattern church.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Lord and Savior.

salvation.position: Hear, believe, repent, confess, be baptized for the remission of sins. Baptism is necessary for salvation.

scripture.position: Bible alone (no creeds). New Testament is the pattern. Silence of Scripture is prohibitive.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. Bodily resurrection.

creation.position: Generally young earth creationist.

angels.position: Real spiritual beings. Standard view.

nephilim.position: Views vary. Not a major focus.

flood.position: Global flood affirmed.

mary.position: Virgin birth. Not venerated.

baptism.position: Immersion only. Adults only. Essential for salvation. 'For the remission of sins' (Acts 2:38).

communion.position: Memorial. Observed every Lord's Day (weekly). Unleavened bread and grape juice.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Cessationist — miraculous gifts ceased with the apostles.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Central to faith.

salvation.position: By grace through faith alone.

scripture.position: Bible as inerrant, authoritative Word of God.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. Views on eschatology vary.

creation.position: God as Creator. Views vary.

angels.position: Real spiritual beings.

nephilim.position: Views vary.

flood.position: Generally affirm historical flood.

mary.position: Virgin birth. Not venerated.

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion. Ordinance.

communion.position: Memorial ordinance.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Views on gifts vary.

god.position: Trinitarian. Absolute sovereignty. 1689 London Baptist Confession.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Mediator of the covenant of grace.

salvation.position: Monergistic. TULIP. Unconditional election. Limited atonement.

scripture.position: Sola scriptura. 66 books. Sufficient and inerrant.

afterlife.position: Heaven and hell. Amillennialism or postmillennialism common.

creation.position: God as Creator. Many hold six-day creation.

angels.position: Real beings under God's sovereignty.

nephilim.position: Views vary. Sethite view common in Reformed circles.

flood.position: Global flood affirmed.

mary.position: Virgin birth. Not venerated. One mediator.

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion only. Not salvific.

communion.position: Spiritual presence. More than mere symbol. Means of grace.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Generally cessationist.

god.position: Trinitarian monotheism.

jesus.position: Fully God, fully man. Currently in heavenly sanctuary performing investigative judgment.

salvation.position: By grace through faith. Investigative judgment since 1844. Obedience to law important but not salvific.

scripture.position: Bible as supreme authority. Ellen White's writings as 'lesser light' pointing to Scripture.

afterlife.position: Soul sleep until resurrection. Annihilationism — wicked destroyed, not eternally tormented. No immortal soul.

creation.position: Six literal days. Young earth. Sabbath as creation memorial.

angels.position: Real spiritual beings. Great controversy between Christ and Satan.

nephilim.position: Generally Sethite view. Ellen White held mixed views.

flood.position: Global flood. Central to SDA geology (catastrophism).

mary.position: Virgin birth. Not venerated.

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion.

communion.position: Memorial. Foot-washing ordinance precedes communion.

holyspirit.position: Third Person of Trinity. Active in the church.

god.position: Absolute monotheism (Tawhid). Allah is one, no Trinity. No partners, no Son. 99 names.

jesus.position: Isa — a prophet and messenger, born of a virgin (Maryam). Not God, not Son of God. Not crucified (a substitute died). Will return.

salvation.position: Submission to Allah. Five Pillars. Good deeds weighed against bad on Day of Judgment. No assurance. No substitutionary atonement.

scripture.position: Quran is final, perfect revelation. Bible (Injil, Torah) was corrupted (tahrif). Quran supersedes all.

afterlife.position: Paradise (Jannah) with physical pleasures. Hellfire (Jahannam) for unbelievers. Bridge (Sirat). Intercession of Muhammad.

creation.position: Allah created heavens and earth in six periods (yawm). Adam created from clay. No evolution.

angels.position: Created from light. Jibril (Gabriel), Mikail (Michael), Israfil, Azrael. No free will — cannot rebel. Jinn are separate.

nephilim.position: No parallel concept. Jinn are the supernatural beings. No angel-human hybridization in Islamic theology.

flood.position: Nuh (Noah) and the flood affirmed in Quran. Details differ from Genesis. Considered historical.

mary.position: Maryam highly honored. Virgin birth of Isa affirmed. Surah 19 dedicated to her. Not divine, not worshipped.

baptism.position: No baptism. Shahada (declaration of faith) is entry into Islam. Ritual washing (wudu) before prayer.

communion.position: No communion. No concept of sacraments. Prayer (salat) and fasting are primary worship acts.

holyspirit.position: Ruh al-Qudus (Holy Spirit) = Angel Jibril. Not a Person of God. Not the Christian Holy Spirit.

god.position: Strict monotheism (Shema). God is one — no Trinity. HaShem. Unknowable essence.

jesus.position: Not the Messiah. A false teacher or at best a moral teacher. Messiah has not yet come.

salvation.position: Torah observance. Tikkun olam (repairing the world). Teshuvah (repentance). Good deeds. No original sin concept.

scripture.position: Tanakh (Hebrew Bible) + Talmud + Midrash. Oral Torah equal to Written Torah. Christian NT rejected entirely.

afterlife.position: Olam Ha-Ba (World to Come). Gehenna is temporary purification (max 12 months). Resurrection of the dead. No eternal hell.

creation.position: God created the world. Views range from literal to allegorical. Some rabbis accept old earth.

angels.position: Mal'akhim (messengers). Michael, Gabriel, Raphael. Angels serve God. Extensive angelology in mystical Judaism.

nephilim.position: Angel view held by many early rabbis (Targumim, Midrash). Some later rabbis adopted human-only view.

flood.position: Affirmed in Torah. Midrashic elaborations. Historical event in Orthodox Judaism.

mary.position: Not relevant. If discussed, considered mother of a false messiah claimant.

baptism.position: Mikveh (ritual immersion) for purification, not salvation. Proselyte baptism for converts.

communion.position: No communion. Shabbat meal, Passover Seder, kiddush over wine are the ritual meals.

holyspirit.position: Ruach HaKodesh — God's spirit/breath, not a separate Person. Prophetic inspiration, not a Trinity member.

god.position: Brahman is ultimate reality. Manifests as many gods (polytheistic practice, monistic philosophy). Trimurti: Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva.

jesus.position: Some consider Jesus an avatar or enlightened teacher. Not unique Son of God. One of many paths.

salvation.position: Moksha — liberation from cycle of rebirth (samsara). Achieved through karma yoga, bhakti yoga, jnana yoga, raja yoga. Multiple paths.

scripture.position: Vedas, Upanishads, Bhagavad Gita, Puranas, Mahabharata, Ramayana. Many sacred texts. Bible not recognized.

afterlife.position: Reincarnation (samsara) based on karma. Goal is moksha — union with Brahman. No permanent heaven/hell.

creation.position: Cyclical creation and destruction. Brahma creates, Vishnu preserves, Shiva destroys. Multiple creation myths.

angels.position: Devas (celestial beings), asuras (demons), gandharvas, apsaras. Rich spiritual hierarchy but different framework.

nephilim.position: No direct parallel. Rakshasas and asuras are powerful beings. Some flood traditions (Manu) have giant-like beings.

flood.position: Manu and the great flood — preserved by Matsya (fish avatar of Vishnu). Parallel to Noah but within Hindu framework.

mary.position: Not relevant to Hinduism.

baptism.position: No baptism. Ritual bathing in sacred rivers (Ganges) for purification.

communion.position: No communion. Prasad (blessed food offerings) distributed after puja.

holyspirit.position: No equivalent. Atman (individual soul) and Brahman (universal spirit) are key concepts.

god.position: No creator God. Non-theistic or agnostic on God. Focus on enlightenment, not deity.

jesus.position: Not relevant. May be considered a bodhisattva or enlightened teacher by some. Not God.

salvation.position: Nirvana — cessation of suffering through the Eightfold Path. No savior needed. Self-effort and enlightenment.

scripture.position: Tripitaka (Pali Canon), Mahayana Sutras, Tibetan Canon. Bible not recognized.

afterlife.position: Rebirth/reincarnation based on karma. Nirvana is the end of the cycle. No eternal soul (anatta/anatman).

creation.position: No creation doctrine. Universe is beginningless and cyclical. Creation is irrelevant to suffering's cessation.

angels.position: Devas exist in higher realms but are not eternal. They too are subject to rebirth.

nephilim.position: No parallel concept.

flood.position: No flood narrative in core Buddhist texts.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: No baptism. Taking refuge in the Three Jewels (Buddha, Dharma, Sangha) is the initiation.

communion.position: No communion. Dana (giving/almsgiving) and shared meals in monastic settings.

holyspirit.position: No equivalent. Buddha-nature in Mahayana is closest concept but fundamentally different.

god.position: God the Father has a physical body of flesh and bone. Was once a man who achieved godhood. Separate being from Jesus and Holy Spirit (not Trinity).

jesus.position: Literal Son of God (spirit brother of Lucifer). Separate being from the Father. Atoned in Gethsemane and on cross.

salvation.position: Grace + obedience + ordinances + temple work. Three degrees of glory. Potential for exaltation (becoming gods). Universal resurrection.

scripture.position: Bible (KJV, 'as far as it is translated correctly') + Book of Mormon + Doctrine & Covenants + Pearl of Great Price. Living prophets can add.

afterlife.position: Three kingdoms of glory: Celestial, Terrestrial, Telestial. Outer darkness for sons of perdition. Baptism for the dead.

creation.position: God organized (not created from nothing) existing matter. Earth is ~6,000 years old. Adam = Michael the archangel.

angels.position: Angels are resurrected beings or pre-mortal spirits. Moroni appeared to Joseph Smith. No angel/human barrier as in Bible.

nephilim.position: No official position. Genesis 6 not emphasized. Book of Mormon has separate narrative of ancient Americas.

flood.position: Affirmed as global event. Earth was baptized by the flood.

mary.position: Virgin birth. Some past leaders taught God the Father physically fathered Jesus through Mary.

baptism.position: By immersion. By proper priesthood authority. Age 8. Baptism for the dead in temples.

communion.position: Sacrament (bread and water). Weekly observance. Memorial of Christ's atonement. Not real presence.

holyspirit.position: Separate personage from God the Father and Jesus. Does not have a body. Testifies of truth.

god.position: Jehovah is the one true God. No Trinity. Jesus is a created being (Michael the archangel). Holy Spirit is God's active force.

jesus.position: Michael the archangel. First creation of God. Not God. Died on a stake (not cross). Rose as a spirit being, not bodily.

salvation.position: Faith + works + loyalty to the organization (Watchtower). 144,000 go to heaven. Others live on paradise earth.

scripture.position: New World Translation (altered to support JW theology). Bible is God's Word but only properly interpreted by Governing Body.

afterlife.position: No immortal soul. Death = nonexistence. 144,000 anointed go to heaven. Faithful Others live on paradise earth. Wicked annihilated.

creation.position: God created through Jesus. Each 'day' = thousands of years. Accept old earth. Reject evolution.

angels.position: Angels are spirit creatures. Jesus = Michael. Satan is a fallen angel. Demons are fallen angels.

nephilim.position: Angel view: fallen angels materialized bodies, had relations with women, produced Nephilim. Pre-flood only.

flood.position: Global flood affirmed. Historical event. Key to their chronology.

mary.position: Virgin birth. Not venerated. Mother of Jesus but not 'Mother of God.'

baptism.position: Believer's baptism by immersion. Signifies dedication to Jehovah. Required.

communion.position: Memorial once a year (Nisan 14). Only 144,000 anointed partake. Others attend but don't eat/drink.

holyspirit.position: Not a person. God's active force (like electricity). Not part of a Trinity.

god.position: Vague 'Supreme Being' or 'Eighth Dynamic.' No personal God. Thetans (spiritual beings) are the focus.

jesus.position: Implant or false memory from past lives. Not recognized as God or Savior.

salvation.position: Auditing and progressing through Operating Thetan (OT) levels. Clear status. Self-improvement through Dianetics.

scripture.position: L. Ron Hubbard's writings are authoritative. Dianetics, Science of Survival, etc. Bible irrelevant.

afterlife.position: Thetans are immortal. Pick up new bodies after death. No heaven/hell. Past lives accessible through auditing.

creation.position: Xenu narrative (OT III). Thetans have existed for trillions of years. No creator God.

angels.position: No concept of angels. Body thetans (BTs) are attached spiritual entities to be removed.

nephilim.position: No parallel concept.

flood.position: Not addressed. May be considered an 'engram' or past-life implant.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: No baptism. 'Naming ceremony' exists but not baptism.

communion.position: No communion or sacraments.

holyspirit.position: No equivalent.

god.position: Ik Onkar — One God. Formless, timeless, all-pervading. No incarnations. Monotheistic.

jesus.position: Not recognized. Gurus are the authority. Jesus may be considered a holy man but not God.

salvation.position: Mukti — liberation through God's grace (nadar) and devotion (naam simran). Break cycle of reincarnation.

scripture.position: Guru Granth Sahib — eternal living Guru. Bible not recognized.

afterlife.position: Reincarnation based on karma. Liberation means union with God. No permanent heaven/hell.

creation.position: God created all. Hukam (divine order). Creation is an expression of God's will.

angels.position: No central concept of angels. Devtas (deities) exist but are subject to the one God.

nephilim.position: No parallel.

flood.position: Not part of Sikh theology.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: Amrit Sanchar — Sikh initiation ceremony (drinking amrit). Not baptism.

communion.position: Langar — community kitchen. Shared meal for all regardless of background. Not communion.

holyspirit.position: No equivalent. God's light (Jyoti) within all beings.

god.position: One unknowable God. All religions worship the same God. Progressive revelation through manifestations.

jesus.position: A 'Manifestation of God' — one in a series including Moses, Buddha, Muhammad, Baha'u'llah. Not uniquely divine.

salvation.position: Progressive spiritual development through recognizing the current Manifestation (Baha'u'llah). Good works, education, service.

scripture.position: Kitab-i-Aqdas, Kitab-i-Iqan, and other Baha'i writings. Bible seen as partially valid but superseded.

afterlife.position: Soul progresses eternally through spiritual worlds. No bodily resurrection. Heaven/hell are states of nearness/remoteness from God.

creation.position: God created the universe. Evolution accepted. Creation is eternal (God always had creation).

angels.position: Angels are symbolic — represent human virtues and confirmations of the Holy Spirit.

nephilim.position: Allegorical interpretation. No literal nephilim.

flood.position: Symbolic/allegorical. Noah represents a spiritual teacher, not a literal global flood.

mary.position: Respected as mother of Jesus but not central to Baha'i theology.

baptism.position: No baptism. Declaration of faith in Baha'u'llah.

communion.position: No communion or sacraments.

holyspirit.position: Bounty/emanation from God that inspires the Manifestations. Not a Person.

god.position: God is an impersonal force/energy. All is God (pantheism/panentheism). Divine consciousness within all.

jesus.position: An ascended master or enlightened being. 'Christ consciousness' is available to all. Not uniquely God.

salvation.position: Self-realization. Raising consciousness. No sin to be saved from — only ignorance. Many paths to enlightenment.

scripture.position: Many texts valued: Eastern scriptures, channeled writings, A Course in Miracles. Bible cherry-picked or reinterpreted.

afterlife.position: Reincarnation. Astral planes. No judgment. Soul continues evolving through multiple lifetimes.

creation.position: Universe emanated from consciousness. May accept evolution. Creation is ongoing consciousness expansion.

angels.position: Angels, spirit guides, ascended masters, archangels are all accessible. Angel channeling common.

nephilim.position: Some New Agers accept ancient astronaut theory — nephilim as alien-human hybrids.

flood.position: May accept as myth or Atlantis destruction narrative. Not historical biblical event.

mary.position: May honor Mary as divine feminine archetype or goddess figure.

baptism.position: No baptism. Various initiation rituals possible.

communion.position: No formal communion. Shared rituals vary.

holyspirit.position: Cosmic energy. Universal life force. Not a Person of a Trinity.

god.position: Allah appeared in the person of Fard Muhammad. God is a man. The black man is God.

jesus.position: A prophet. Not God. Not crucified/resurrected in the Christian sense. White Christianity distorted his message.

salvation.position: Knowledge of self. Racial consciousness. Separation from white influence. Supreme wisdom.

scripture.position: Quran and Elijah Muhammad's teachings. Bible is seen as corrupted but contains some truth.

afterlife.position: No literal afterlife. Heaven and hell are conditions in this life. Mental resurrection is the goal.

creation.position: Yakub's History: a scientist named Yakub created the white race 6,600 years ago through selective breeding.

angels.position: Symbolic or non-standard interpretation. Not traditional Islamic angelology.

nephilim.position: No standard position.

flood.position: Not emphasized in NOI theology.

mary.position: Not a focus.

baptism.position: No baptism.

communion.position: No communion.

holyspirit.position: Not a central concept.

god.position: God is Mind, Spirit, Soul, Principle, Life, Truth, Love. Not a person. All is Mind.

jesus.position: Jesus demonstrated the Christ (divine idea). Distinction between Jesus (human) and Christ (divine). Not God incarnate.

salvation.position: Understanding that sin, sickness, and death are illusions of mortal mind. Salvation is awakening to spiritual truth.

scripture.position: Bible + Science and Health with Key to the Scriptures (Mary Baker Eddy). Bible interpreted through Eddy's lens.

afterlife.position: No death in reality. Consciousness continues to progress. No literal heaven/hell.

creation.position: Spiritual creation only. Material world is an illusion (mortal mind). True creation is spiritual and perfect.

angels.position: Angels are God's thoughts or spiritual ideas. Not literal beings.

nephilim.position: Allegorical. Material interpretation rejected.

flood.position: Symbolic/allegorical.

mary.position: Virgin birth acknowledged spiritually. Mary received a 'spiritual idea.'

baptism.position: Spiritual baptism only. No water baptism.

communion.position: Spiritual communion through silent prayer. No bread/cup. Twice yearly 'communion season.'

holyspirit.position: Divine Science. Christian Science itself. Not a Person.

god.position: No required belief in God. Members may be theist, deist, pantheist, agnostic, atheist, or humanist.

jesus.position: A moral teacher. Not God. Not divine. One of many great human teachers.

salvation.position: No doctrine of salvation. Universal inherent worth. Focus on justice, equity, and compassion.

scripture.position: No authoritative scripture. Many sources drawn from: Bible, world religions, science, humanist writings.

afterlife.position: No official position. Many believe this life is all there is. Some believe in ongoing consciousness.

creation.position: Generally accept scientific consensus (evolution, Big Bang). No doctrine.

angels.position: No doctrine of angels. Symbolic at most.

nephilim.position: Mythology.

flood.position: Mythology/legend.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: Child dedication ceremony (not baptism). No required rituals.

communion.position: Flower communion or water communion (symbolic community rituals). Not Christian communion.

holyspirit.position: No doctrine. May be interpreted as human aspiration or community spirit.

god.position: Ahura Mazda — one supreme God of wisdom and light. Dualistic struggle with Angra Mainyu (evil spirit).

jesus.position: Not recognized. Zarathustra is the prophet.

salvation.position: Good thoughts, good words, good deeds (Humata, Hukhta, Hvarshta). Choose Asha (truth) over Druj (lie).

scripture.position: Avesta (Gathas, Yasna, Vendidad). Bible not recognized.

afterlife.position: Chinvat Bridge judgment. Heaven (House of Song) or hell (House of Lies). Final renovation (Frashokereti) — evil destroyed.

creation.position: Ahura Mazda created a perfect world. Angra Mainyu corrupted it. Seven creations.

angels.position: Amesha Spentas (Holy Immortals) — seven archangel-like beings. Yazatas (worthy of worship). Rich angelology.

nephilim.position: No direct parallel. Daeva (demons) are fallen spiritual beings.

flood.position: Vara of Yima — Yima builds an enclosure (vara) to protect life from a great winter/catastrophe. Parallel to Ark.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: Navjote initiation (investiture with sacred shirt and cord). Not baptism.

communion.position: Yasna ceremony with haoma drink. Ritual parallels but different theology.

holyspirit.position: Spenta Mainyu (Holy Spirit/Bounteous Spirit) — Ahura Mazda's creative spirit. Possible influence on Jewish/Christian concept.

god.position: The Tao (Way) — impersonal, nameless, formless principle underlying all reality. Not a personal God.

jesus.position: Not recognized. Lao Tzu and Chuang Tzu are the sages.

salvation.position: Harmony with the Tao. Wu wei (non-action/effortless action). Simplicity. Return to natural state.

scripture.position: Tao Te Ching, Chuang Tzu, I Ching. Bible not recognized.

afterlife.position: Physical immortality sought in religious Taoism. Philosophical Taoism: dissolution back into the Tao. No judgment.

creation.position: The Tao produced the One, the One produced the Two, the Two produced the Three, the Three produced all things.

angels.position: Immortals (Xian) and deities in Taoist pantheon. Not biblical angels.

nephilim.position: No parallel.

flood.position: Yu the Great tamed the flood — Chinese flood narrative. Historical/mythological.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: No baptism. Purification rituals exist.

communion.position: No communion. Offerings to deities.

holyspirit.position: Chi/Qi — life energy. Not a Person.

god.position: Kami — countless spirits/deities in nature, ancestors, and sacred places. Polytheistic/animistic.

jesus.position: Not recognized.

salvation.position: Purity and harmony. Ritual purification (harae). No concept of sin in the Christian sense. Pollution (kegare) vs purity.

scripture.position: Kojiki and Nihon Shoki (mythological histories). Not scripture in Western sense. Bible not recognized.

afterlife.position: Yomi (underworld). Ancestors become kami. Focus is on this life. Death is pollution.

creation.position: Izanagi and Izanami created the Japanese islands. National creation myth.

angels.position: Kami serve various roles. Not angels in the biblical sense.

nephilim.position: No parallel.

flood.position: No central flood narrative.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: Misogi (ritual purification under waterfall). Not baptism.

communion.position: Naorai (communal drinking of sake after ritual). Not communion.

holyspirit.position: No equivalent. Musubi (creative force) is closest.

god.position: Jah — God incarnate in Haile Selassie I. Immanent God. African-centered theology.

jesus.position: A black messiah figure. Haile Selassie is the returned Christ (Revelation 5:5, Lion of Judah).

salvation.position: Repatriation to Africa (Zion). Liberation from Babylon (Western oppression). Ital living.

scripture.position: King James Bible (selectively interpreted). Holy Piby. Kebra Nagast. Bible seen as corrupted by whites.

afterlife.position: No death for the righteous. Eternal life in the flesh. Some believe in reincarnation.

creation.position: God created all. Africa is the Promised Land. Ethiopia is Eden.

angels.position: Not a major focus. Spiritual beings acknowledged.

nephilim.position: Not a focus.

flood.position: Acknowledged from Bible but not central.

mary.position: Not a focus.

baptism.position: No formal baptism.

communion.position: No formal communion. Groundation (Nyabinghi) ceremonies.

holyspirit.position: Jah's presence in each person. Not distinct from God.

god.position: YHWH is the God of the Bible. Some groups are Trinitarian, some are not.

jesus.position: Yahawashi — a black man. The Messiah. Came for the 'lost tribes' (black people, Hispanics, Native Americans).

salvation.position: Return to Torah observance. Keeping the commandments. Ethnic identity as Israelites. Law-keeping essential.

scripture.position: Bible (especially KJV). Apocrypha included. Some use the 'original Hebrew' names movement.

afterlife.position: Varies by group. Some affirm resurrection. Some conditional immortality.

creation.position: God created all. Genesis literal.

angels.position: Biblical angelology accepted. Fallen angels real.

nephilim.position: Angel view common. Genesis 6 taken literally.

flood.position: Global flood affirmed.

mary.position: A black Israelite woman. Not venerated.

baptism.position: Varies. Some practice immersion.

communion.position: Varies by group. Passover observed.

holyspirit.position: Varies. Some Trinitarian, some view as God's power.

god.position: Great Architect of the Universe (GAOTU). Non-specific deity. All religions welcome. Higher degrees: Jahbulon (composite name).

jesus.position: Not mentioned in ritual. In higher degrees, Jesus is one of many teachers alongside Zoroaster, Confucius, etc.

salvation.position: Moral improvement. 'Building the Temple.' Immortality through good character. No need for atonement.

scripture.position: Volume of Sacred Law (VSL) — any religious book. Bible used in Christian lodges but not authoritative.

afterlife.position: Celestial Lodge Above. Immortality of the soul. No judgment or hell.

creation.position: GAOTU created all. Details left to individual belief.

angels.position: Not part of Masonic teaching. Some higher degree rituals reference spiritual beings.

nephilim.position: Not addressed.

flood.position: Referenced in York Rite (Royal Arch). Noah as a Masonic figure. Largely symbolic.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: No baptism. Initiation rituals (degrees) replace.

communion.position: Table Lodge (festive board) has ritual toasts. Not communion.

holyspirit.position: Not part of Masonic teaching.

god.position: The true God is the Monad/Pleroma — unknowable, transcendent. The creator God (Demiurge/Yaldabaoth) is ignorant or evil.

jesus.position: A divine Aeon sent from the Pleroma to bring gnosis (knowledge). Did not truly suffer or die (Docetism). Christ spirit left before crucifixion.

salvation.position: Gnosis — hidden knowledge of one's divine origin. Escape from the material prison. Not faith or works but awakening.

scripture.position: Nag Hammadi texts: Gospel of Thomas, Gospel of Philip, Pistis Sophia, etc. Orthodox Bible = Demiurge's propaganda.

afterlife.position: Soul returns to Pleroma if gnosis is achieved. Otherwise trapped in reincarnation by archons.

creation.position: Material world is a flawed creation by the Demiurge (often identified with YHWH). Creation is a prison, not good.

angels.position: Archons are negative spiritual rulers. Aeons are emanations of the true God. Complex spiritual hierarchy.

nephilim.position: Archons interacting with humans is a common Gnostic theme. Material corruption through spiritual fall.

flood.position: Some Gnostic texts invert the narrative — the Demiurge tried to destroy enlightened humans.

mary.position: Mary Magdalene elevated as primary disciple and holder of secret teachings. Virgin Mary secondary.

baptism.position: Bridal chamber ritual (five Gnostic sacraments). Water baptism may be practiced. Spiritual baptism emphasized.

communion.position: Some Gnostic groups had their own eucharist with different theology.

holyspirit.position: Sophia (Wisdom) or the divine feminine. Part of the Pleroma's emanation hierarchy.

god.position: Ein Sof — the infinite, unknowable God. Ten Sefirot as emanations/attributes of God. Tree of Life. Panentheistic.

jesus.position: Not recognized. Jewish mystical framework. Some Christian Cabala later incorporated Christ.

salvation.position: Tikkun (repair) of the cosmos through mitzvot, meditation, and elevating sparks of divine light trapped in matter.

scripture.position: Torah (especially mystical interpretations), Zohar, Sefer Yetzirah, writings of Ari (Luria).

afterlife.position: Gilgul (reincarnation). Soul repairs itself through multiple lives. Gehenna for purification.

creation.position: Tzimtzum — God contracted to make space for creation. Shevirat ha-Kelim (shattering of vessels). Sparks scattered.

angels.position: Rich angelology. Angels associated with each Sefirah. Metatron, Sandalphon, etc. Can be invoked.

nephilim.position: Some Kabbalistic texts discuss fallen angels (Aza and Azael). Zohar has references to angelic fall.

flood.position: Symbolic and mystical interpretations alongside literal. The flood represents cosmic purification.

mary.position: Not relevant in Jewish Kabbalah.

baptism.position: Mikveh (ritual immersion) with mystical significance. Represents return to primordial waters.

communion.position: Shabbat meal has mystical significance. Kiddush unites upper and lower worlds.

holyspirit.position: Shekhinah — feminine divine presence. Ruach HaKodesh as prophetic level below full prophecy.

god.position: Impersonal Absolute. No personal God. Universal law and consciousness.

jesus.position: An initiate/adept who achieved high spiritual status. Not uniquely God. One of many masters.

salvation.position: Evolution of consciousness through reincarnation. Karma. Self-perfection over many lives.

scripture.position: The Secret Doctrine, Isis Unveiled (Blavatsky). All religious texts have hidden truths.

afterlife.position: Devachan (heaven-like state between lives). Reincarnation. Nirvana is ultimate goal.

creation.position: Emanation from the Absolute. Seven rounds of evolution. Root races. Cosmic evolution.

angels.position: Ascended Masters. Planetary spirits. Devas. Hierarchies of beings.

nephilim.position: Part of root race theory. Atlantean and Lemurian races. Giants existed.

flood.position: Destruction of Atlantis. Historical geological event reinterpreted.

mary.position: Isis/divine feminine archetype.

baptism.position: No baptism. Initiation into mysteries.

communion.position: No communion.

holyspirit.position: Fohat/universal life force. Not a Person.

god.position: No God. Atheistic. Satan is a symbol of individualism, pride, and human nature, not a real being.

jesus.position: Rejected. Christianity seen as weakness-promoting and life-denying.

salvation.position: No salvation needed. Self-deification. Indulgence over abstinence. This life is all there is.

scripture.position: The Satanic Bible (LaVey). Bible is rejected.

afterlife.position: No afterlife. Live fully now. No soul. Death is the end.

creation.position: Accepts scientific consensus. No creator deity.

angels.position: Demons are symbolic. Names of demons invoked ritually but not believed to be real (atheistic).

nephilim.position: Mythology.

flood.position: Mythology.

mary.position: Irrelevant. Mocked.

baptism.position: No baptism. Un-baptism ritual exists.

communion.position: Black Mass parodies communion.

holyspirit.position: Does not exist.

god.position: The All — mind/consciousness. 'The All is Mind; the Universe is Mental.' Seven Hermetic principles.

jesus.position: Not central. Some Christian Hermeticists see Christ as embodying Hermetic truth.

salvation.position: Gnosis of the All. Understanding the seven Hermetic principles. Mental alchemy. Transmutation.

scripture.position: Corpus Hermeticum, The Kybalion, Emerald Tablet.

afterlife.position: Soul ascends through planetary spheres back to the All. Reincarnation possible.

creation.position: 'As above, so below.' Creation emanates from the Mind of the All through vibration.

angels.position: Planetary spirits, daemons. Spiritual hierarchies.

nephilim.position: Not directly addressed.

flood.position: Not central.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: Spiritual rebirth through gnosis. 'Baptism in Nous (Mind).'

communion.position: No formal communion.

holyspirit.position: Nous (Divine Mind). Not a Person in the Christian sense.

god.position: Universal Cosmic Consciousness. God as impersonal principle. All religions contain truth.

jesus.position: A high initiate. Christ principle is universal. Not uniquely God incarnate.

salvation.position: Self-mastery, spiritual evolution, inner alchemy. Knowledge + practice = illumination.

scripture.position: Rosicrucian manifestos, Cosmo-Conception (Heindel). Bible seen as esoteric allegory.

afterlife.position: Reincarnation. Desire World, Thought World. Soul progresses.

creation.position: Seven Periods of cosmic evolution. Spiritual and material planes.

angels.position: Archangels, Angels, and other hierarchies are part of cosmic evolution.

nephilim.position: Part of fallen spiritual hierarchy.

flood.position: Atlantean catastrophe. Spiritual evolution narrative.

mary.position: Divine feminine principle.

baptism.position: Symbolic initiation.

communion.position: No formal communion.

holyspirit.position: Universal Life Spirit.

god.position: Polytheistic pantheon. Marduk supreme in Babylon. Mother goddess worship (Ishtar/Inanna). Self-deification.

jesus.position: No concept. Tammuz (dying-and-rising god) seen by some as Satanic counterfeit of Christ.

salvation.position: Mystery initiation rites. Secret knowledge. Ritual death and rebirth. Ascent through spiritual levels.

scripture.position: Enuma Elish, Epic of Gilgamesh, various ritual texts.

afterlife.position: Underworld (Kur/Irkalla). Gloomy realm of the dead. Ishtar's descent narrative.

creation.position: Marduk slays Tiamat (chaos dragon) and creates world from her body. Humans made to serve gods.

angels.position: Anunnaki — the great gods/judges. Igigi — lesser gods. Apkallu — sages. Rich spiritual hierarchy.

nephilim.position: Apkallu (pre-flood sages) are human-divine hybrids in some traditions. Gilgamesh was 2/3 god, 1/3 human.

flood.position: Utnapishtim/Ziusudra flood narrative. Very similar to Genesis. Predates written Torah.

mary.position: Semiramis/Ishtar — Queen of Heaven. Madonna and child motif (Semiramis and Tammuz).

baptism.position: Ritual purification. Water rites in temple rituals.

communion.position: Sacred meals. Bread and wine offerings to gods.

holyspirit.position: No equivalent. Me (divine powers/decrees) is closest concept.

god.position: Polytheistic. Zeus supreme. Olympian pantheon. Gods are powerful but flawed, not omnipotent or omnibenevolent.

jesus.position: Not applicable. Dionysus (dying-and-rising god) and Heracles (divine-human) are noted parallels/counterfeits.

salvation.position: No salvation concept. Fate (Moirai) rules. Virtuous life earns Elysium. Mystery cults offered hope.

scripture.position: Homer's Iliad and Odyssey. Hesiod's Theogony and Works & Days. Not 'scripture' in biblical sense.

afterlife.position: Hades (underworld). Elysium (blessed), Tartarus (punishment), Asphodel Meadows (ordinary). River Styx.

creation.position: Chaos → Gaia → Titans → Olympians. Prometheus creates humans from clay.

angels.position: Daimons — intermediate spiritual beings. Nike (victory), Eros (love), etc. Not angels but some parallels.

nephilim.position: Demigods (Heracles, Perseus, Achilles) — offspring of gods and human women. Direct parallel to Genesis 6.

flood.position: Deucalion and Pyrrha flood. Zeus destroys corrupt humanity. Very similar structure to Genesis.

mary.position: Not applicable.

baptism.position: Lustral water purification. Mystery cult initiations.

communion.position: Libations. Symposia. Mystery cult meals (Eleusinian).

holyspirit.position: Pneuma (breath/spirit). Stoic concept. Not a Person.

god.position: Polytheistic. Odin All-Father supreme. Aesir and Vanir gods. Gods are mortal (Ragnarok).

jesus.position: Not applicable. Balder (innocent god who dies and will return) is noted parallel.

salvation.position: No salvation. Fate (Wyrd). Honorable death in battle → Valhalla. Ragnarok → renewal.

scripture.position: Poetic Edda, Prose Edda (Snorri Sturluson). Sagas.

afterlife.position: Valhalla (warriors), Folkvangr (Freya's hall), Hel (realm of the dead), Niflheim. Ragnarok → rebirth.

creation.position: Ginnungagap (void). Ymir (frost giant) slain, world made from his body. Ask and Embla (first humans) from trees.

angels.position: Valkyries, Norns (fate), light elves. Not angels but spiritual beings.

nephilim.position: Giants (Jotnar) are a primordial race. Gods and giants interbreed. Direct parallel to Nephilim concept.

flood.position: Bergelmir survived the flood of Ymir's blood. Frost giant deluge narrative parallels Genesis.

mary.position: Not applicable. Frigg is queen of Asgard.

baptism.position: Water ritual (Vatni Ausa) — sprinkling newborns. Name-giving ceremony.

communion.position: Blot — sacrifice and communal feast. Mead of poetry.

holyspirit.position: Odin's breath gave life to Ask and Embla. Not a Person.

god.position: Polytheistic. Ra/Amun-Ra supreme. Complex pantheon. Pharaoh is divine. Some monotheistic elements (Atenism).

jesus.position: Not applicable. Osiris (dying-and-rising god) and Horus (divine son) are noted parallels/counterfeits.

salvation.position: Ma'at (cosmic order/justice). Heart weighed against feather in judgment. Living rightly according to Ma'at.

scripture.position: Book of the Dead (Book of Coming Forth by Day). Pyramid Texts. Coffin Texts.

afterlife.position: Duat (underworld). Judgment by Osiris. Heart weighed. Field of Reeds (paradise). Ammit devours wicked.

creation.position: Atum/Ra spoke creation into being from primordial waters (Nun). Parallels to Genesis 1.

angels.position: Netjeru (gods/goddesses) and lesser spirits. Not angels but complex spiritual hierarchy.

nephilim.position: Pharaohs as sons of gods. Divine-human offspring concept present.

flood.position: Ra sends Hathor/Sekhmet to destroy humanity (partial parallel). Beer dyed red saves remnant.

mary.position: Isis nursing Horus — prototype of Madonna and Child imagery.

baptism.position: Ritual purification in sacred lakes before temple entry.

communion.position: Offerings of bread, beer, meat to gods. Shared meals.

holyspirit.position: Ka (life force), Ba (personality/soul), Akh (transfigured spirit). Not equivalent.

god.position: Polytheistic. An (heaven), Enlil (air), Enki (water), Inanna (love/war). Council of gods (divine assembly).

jesus.position: Not applicable. Dumuzi (Tammuz) dying-and-rising god is noted parallel.

salvation.position: No salvation concept. Serve the gods. Fate determined by divine decree (NAM.TAR).

scripture.position: Cuneiform tablets. Enuma Elish, Atrahasis, Epic of Gilgamesh.

afterlife.position: Kur/Irkalla — dark, gloomy underworld for all. No reward. 'Land of no return.'

creation.position: Humans created from clay mixed with divine blood to serve gods. Parallels to Genesis 2 (Adam from dust).

angels.position: Anunnaki (great gods), Igigi (lesser gods/workers), Apkallu (seven sages sent to teach humanity).

nephilim.position: Apkallu (divine sages) brought forbidden knowledge. Gilgamesh 2/3 divine. Direct parallel tradition.

flood.position: Atrahasis and Ziusudra flood. Gods decide to destroy humanity with flood. One righteous man saved. Very close to Genesis.

mary.position: Inanna/Ishtar as Queen of Heaven. Fertility goddess.

baptism.position: Ritual purification with water (apsu/abzu).

communion.position: Sacred meals. Bread and beer offerings.

holyspirit.position: Me — divine decrees/powers. Not a Person.

god.position: Shangdi (Supreme Emperor) or Tian (Heaven). Polytheistic practice with ancestor worship. Confucian, Taoist, Buddhist blend.

jesus.position: Not recognized. Confucius and Lao Tzu are the sages.

salvation.position: Harmony with Tao/Heaven. Filial piety. Virtue. Ancestor veneration.

scripture.position: Analects (Confucius), Tao Te Ching, I Ching, various Buddhist sutras.

afterlife.position: Ancestor realm. Diyu (hell courts). Reincarnation. Ghost festivals. Complex afterlife geography.

creation.position: Pangu broke cosmic egg. Nuwa created humans from clay. Various regional myths.

angels.position: Shen (gods/spirits). Celestial bureaucracy. Not biblical angels.

nephilim.position: No direct parallel. Dragon kings and divine-human offspring in mythology.

flood.position: Gun-Yu flood. Yu the Great spent 13 years taming the great flood. Major flood narrative.

mary.position: Not relevant. Guanyin (goddess of mercy) fills similar cultural role.

baptism.position: No baptism. Purification rituals exist.

communion.position: Ancestral offerings. Communal feasts. Not communion.

holyspirit.position: Qi (life energy). Tian (Heaven's will). Not a Person.

god.position: Brahman manifests as Trimurti: Brahma (creator), Vishnu (preserver), Shiva (destroyer). Millions of deities.

jesus.position: Some consider Jesus an avatar of Vishnu. Not unique.

salvation.position: Moksha through dharma, karma, bhakti. Multiple paths over multiple lives.

scripture.position: Vedas, Upanishads, Mahabharata, Ramayana, Puranas, Bhagavad Gita.

afterlife.position: Reincarnation (samsara). Karma determines next life. Moksha is liberation.

creation.position: Cosmic egg. Brahma creates. Cycles of creation and destruction (kalpas). Vishnu sleeps on cosmic ocean.

angels.position: Devas (gods), Asuras (demons), Gandharvas (celestial musicians), Apsaras (celestial dancers).

nephilim.position: Rakshasas (demon-like giants). Gods mating with humans (Krishna's many offspring).

flood.position: Manu warned by Matsya (fish avatar). Builds boat. Very similar to Noah narrative.

mary.position: Not relevant. Durga/Parvati/Lakshmi are divine feminine.

baptism.position: Sacred river bathing (Ganges). Jala Snana.

communion.position: Prasad — blessed food from deity.

holyspirit.position: Atman/Brahman. Universal soul. Not a Person.

god.position: Great Spirit (Unetlanvhi). Creator of all. Generally monotheistic with animistic elements.

jesus.position: Not part of traditional belief. Some syncretism with Christianity post-contact.

salvation.position: Harmony and balance. Purification ceremonies. Living in right relationship with creation.

scripture.position: Oral tradition. No written scripture.

afterlife.position: Nightland (western spirit world). Ancestors continue.

creation.position: Water world. Great Buzzard shaped the mountains. Water Beetle brought mud up.

angels.position: Spiritual beings in nature. Thunder Beings. Animal spirits.

nephilim.position: Giant stories in oral tradition (Judaculla). Stone giants.

flood.position: Flood narratives present. Water covered the world. Animals and a remnant survived.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: Going to water (ritual cleansing in rivers). Purification ceremonies.

communion.position: Green Corn Ceremony. Communal feast with spiritual significance.

holyspirit.position: Great Spirit's presence in all things. Not a Person.

god.position: Taiowa (Creator) and Sotuknang (his helper). Creator deity with spiritual hierarchy.

jesus.position: Not part of traditional belief. Pahana (True White Brother) prophesied to return — some parallel Christ.

salvation.position: Living the Hopi Way (humble, peaceful). Following Creator's plan. Purification.

scripture.position: Oral tradition. Prophecy tablets. Petroglyphs.

afterlife.position: Return to the underworld. Kachina spirits. Multiple worlds of existence.

creation.position: Four Worlds. Humanity climbed through three underworlds to the Fourth World (current). Destruction of previous worlds.

angels.position: Kachina spirits — intermediaries between humans and gods. Over 400 named.

nephilim.position: Giants existed in earlier worlds. Destroyed for wickedness.

flood.position: Third World destroyed by water (flood). Hopi survived in reed boats or by hiding in ant mounds.

mary.position: Not relevant. Spider Woman is feminine creator figure.

baptism.position: Naming ceremony with corn meal and water.

communion.position: Ceremonial meals associated with kachina dances.

holyspirit.position: Breath of life from Creator. Not a Person.

god.position: Wakan Tanka (Great Mystery/Great Spirit). Creator. Encompasses all spiritual powers.

jesus.position: Not part of traditional belief. White Buffalo Calf Woman brought the sacred pipe.

salvation.position: Living in harmony. Vision Quest. Sun Dance. Sweat Lodge purification.

scripture.position: Oral tradition. Sacred stories passed down.

afterlife.position: Spirit world. Milky Way as path of spirits. Ancestors.

creation.position: Inyan (rock) and the creation of the four directions. Sky and earth separation.

angels.position: Spirits in all things. Thunder Beings (Wakinyan). Not angels but spiritual beings.

nephilim.position: Giant stories in oral tradition.

flood.position: Great flood narrative present. Unktehi (water monster) caused flood. Saved on hilltop.

mary.position: Not relevant. White Buffalo Calf Woman is sacred feminine figure.

baptism.position: Inipi (sweat lodge) purification. Not baptism.

communion.position: Pipe ceremony. Communal sharing of sacred smoke.

holyspirit.position: Wakan (sacred/holy) pervades all things. Not a Person.

god.position: Creator (Shonkwaya'tihson). Good-minded Twin vs. Evil-minded Twin dualism.

jesus.position: Not part of traditional belief. Peacemaker brought the Great Law.

salvation.position: Following the Great Law of Peace. Thanksgiving. Harmony.

scripture.position: Oral tradition. Wampum records.

afterlife.position: Sky World. Spirit journey. Good and bad afterlife destinations.

creation.position: Sky Woman falls from Sky World. Lands on turtle's back (Turtle Island). Twin sons create world.

angels.position: Sky dwellers. Three Sisters (corn, beans, squash) as spiritual gifts.

nephilim.position: Stone Giants (Ot-ne-yar-hed) in oral tradition.

flood.position: Sky Woman narrative involves water world. Flood and creation intertwined.

mary.position: Not relevant. Sky Woman is divine feminine.

baptism.position: No baptism. Water rituals for purification.

communion.position: Thanksgiving feast. Strawberry ceremony.

holyspirit.position: Orenda — spiritual power in all things. Not a Person.

god.position: Gitche Manitou (Great Spirit). Creator. Manitou (spirit) in all things.

jesus.position: Not part of traditional belief.

salvation.position: Harmony with nature. Honoring the Great Spirit. Purification.

scripture.position: Oral tradition.

afterlife.position: Happy Hunting Ground. Spirit world.

creation.position: Great Spirit created the world. Earth-diver creation (animal brings mud from water).

angels.position: Manitou spirits. Windigo. Thunderbird.

nephilim.position: Giant stories (Windigo, stone giants).

flood.position: Great flood narrative. Nanabozho/Wisakedjak and the flood. Animals help recreate earth.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: No baptism. Water ceremonies.

communion.position: Feast ceremonies.

holyspirit.position: Manitou — spiritual power in all things.

god.position: Kishelamakank (Creator). Great Manito. All things have spirit.

jesus.position: Not part of traditional belief.

salvation.position: Right living. Ceremonial renewal. Big House Ceremony.

scripture.position: Walum Olum (debated). Oral tradition.

afterlife.position: Spirit world. Twelve levels of heaven in some traditions.

creation.position: Great Turtle carries the earth. Creation from primordial waters.

angels.position: Manito spirits throughout nature.

nephilim.position: Giant traditions.

flood.position: Great flood in Walum Olum. Waters covered the land. People survived.

mary.position: Not relevant.

baptism.position: No baptism. Sweat lodge ceremonies.

communion.position: Big House Ceremony communal meals.

holyspirit.position: Manito — spiritual power.

god.position: YHWH alone is God. Angels are created servants.

jesus.position: The Angel of YHWH in the OT is a Christophany — pre-incarnate Christ.

salvation.position: Angels do not need salvation. They minister to the heirs of salvation.

scripture.position: Angels are revealed in Scripture and confirmed in extrabiblical Second Temple literature.

afterlife.position: Angels escort believers (Luke 16:22). Will be present at final judgment.

creation.position: Angels were present at creation (Job 38:7). Created before the earth.

angels.position: Seraphim, Cherubim, Ophanim, Archangels (Michael, Gabriel), Watchers, Holy Ones, Sons of God. Council of YHWH.

nephilim.position: Watchers descended to Mount Hermon (1 Enoch 6). Had children with human women. Bound until judgment.

flood.position: Angelic corruption was a primary cause of the flood (Genesis 6, 1 Enoch 10).

mary.position: Gabriel announced the birth of Jesus to Mary.

baptism.position: Not directly related to angels.

communion.position: Not directly related to angels.

holyspirit.position: The Spirit is distinct from angels. Angels serve; the Spirit indwells.
